top_stories.title: Entra passkey enrollment vishing targets Microsoft 365 users
top_stories.summary: A threat actor is conducting vishing (voice phishing) campaigns targeting Microsoft 365 users across multiple sectors, tricking them into enrolling attacker-controlled passkeys in Microsoft Entra. The attack exploits the passkey enrollment process in Microsoft Entra ID to gain persistent authentication access to victim accounts.

top_stories.title: BeyondTrust Patches Critical Auth Bypass Flaws in Remote Support and PRA
top_stories.summary: BeyondTrust has released patches for two critical security vulnerabilities affecting its Remote Support and Privileged Remote Access (PRA) products. One flaw, CVE-2026-40138, carries a CVSS score of 9.2 and allows unauthenticated attackers to take control of affected devices without prior authentication.

top_stories.title: Barracuda adds PAM and identity protection with Evo Security acquisition
top_stories.summary: Barracuda Networks has acquired Evo Security to expand its identity security capabilities. The deal adds privileged access management (PAM), access control, identity protection, and identity threat detection and response to Barracuda's BarracudaONE platform.

top_stories.title: Lone Attacker Uses AI to Breach AWS Cloud Environment in 72 Hours
top_stories.summary: A lone attacker breached an AWS cloud environment within 72 hours by exploiting AI workflows, chaining cloud misconfigurations, and using stolen credentials. The attack culminated in an extortion attempt against a large Amazon customer, highlighting credential theft and cloud access exploitation as key vectors.

top_stories.title: Blackpoint AI SOC Agent autonomously contains identity-based attacks
top_stories.summary: Blackpoint Cyber has launched the generally available AI SOC Agent, an autonomous identity threat detection and response (ITDR) capability for Microsoft 365 and Google Workspace. The tool uses an AI and human hybrid model to contain credential-based attacks in under two minutes on average, and as fast as 21 seconds.

news.title: 3 Ways AI Powers Service Desk Attacks and How to Prevent Them
news.summary: Specops Software has published guidance on how AI is enabling more sophisticated service desk impersonation attacks through increased personalization and scalability. The article covers practical defensive measures focused on identity verification and onboarding processes at the service desk level.

news.title: Telco giant KDDI says data breach affects over 12 million people
news.summary: Japanese telecom KDDI disclosed a data breach exposing email addresses and passwords of over 12 million people via a compromised email platform used by five ISPs. The breach involved credential data, making it directly relevant to authentication and identity security concerns.

news.title: Attackers using Langflow flaw for credential harvesting (CVE-2026-55255)
news.summary: CISA has added CVE-2026-55255, a Langflow vulnerability being actively exploited for credential harvesting, to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. Langflow is an open-source visual framework for building AI agents and workflows, and the flaw was observed being actively targeted by the Sysdig Threat Research Team nearly two weeks before the catalog addition.

news.title: The Verification Step Is the New ATO Battleground in 2026
news.summary: An analysis piece examines the evolution of account takeover (ATO) attacks as passkeys become mainstream, shifting the battleground from credential stuffing to the verification step itself. The article addresses how attackers are adapting their methods as traditional authentication defenses improve, covering themes of authentication, identity verification, and emerging attack vectors relevant to IAM practitioners.

news.title: SailPoint、「Agentic Acceleration」を発表、時間を要するクラウド モダナイゼーションを、迅速なシステム導入へと変革
news.summary: SailPoint announced "Agentic Acceleration," a new initiative aimed at transforming cloud modernization for identity management systems. The announcement was originally made on June 16, 2026, with this being a Japanese-language summary of the US press release.

news.title: Big Brand Jobs Scam Targets Marketing Pros' Google Accounts
news.summary: A phishing campaign is targeting marketing professionals with the goal of stealing Google account credentials. The attack uses nested redirects and other evasion techniques to bypass detection while impersonating big brand job offers.

news.title: DEBULL Tooling Abuses Microsoft Device-Code Flow to Target M365 Accounts
news.summary: A phishing campaign dubbed DEBULL has been observed abusing Microsoft's device-code authentication flow to compromise Microsoft 365 accounts, active from late June into early July 2026. The attack bypasses traditional credential-harvesting methods by manipulating the legitimate Microsoft device login experience through collaboration-themed lures, as reported by ZeroBEC.

news.title: Court Filing Reveals Windows Device ID Helped FBI Trace Alleged Scattered Spider Hacker
news.summary: A newly unsealed federal complaint reveals that the FBI used a persistent Windows device ID from Microsoft records to link an alleged Scattered Spider member to a cyberattack on a luxury jewelry retailer. The case illustrates how device identity artifacts and Microsoft account records were used as forensic evidence to attribute the intrusion to 19-year-old Peter Stokes.

news.title: Writer AI Flaw Could Let Agent Previews Leak Session Tokens Across Tenants
news.summary: Researchers disclosed a now-patched critical session isolation vulnerability in Writer's enterprise AI platform that could allow cross-tenant session token leakage. The flaw, dubbed WriteOut by Sand Security Research, could enable an unauthenticated outsider to take over any Writer AI tenant via a one-click attack.

news.title: INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf of Investors of SailPoint, Inc. - SAIL
news.summary: Pomerantz LLP has announced an investigation into claims on behalf of investors of SailPoint, Inc. (NASDAQ: SAIL). The notice advises affected investors to contact the law firm but provides no details about the nature of the claims being investigated.

news.title: Keeper Security Extends Agentic AI Governance to Endpoint Privilege Manager
news.summary: Keeper Security has extended its agentic AI governance capabilities to its Endpoint Privilege Manager, enabling discovery and governance of AI agents on employee workstations and endpoints. The update introduces access policies and elevation controls for AI agents operating at the endpoint level, framed within Keeper's zero-trust and zero-knowledge security model.

news.title: Microsoft to enable Windows settings backup by default for enterprise devices
news.summary: Microsoft will enable Windows settings backup and restore by default starting with Windows 11 version 26H2 for enterprise devices joined to Microsoft Entra. The change transitions the feature from opt-in to default-enabled on Entra-joined systems.

news.title: Privileged Access Management Market Size to Hit $22.93 Billion by 2035 | SNS Insider
news.summary: SNS Insider projects the global Privileged Access Management market will reach $22.93 billion by 2035, with the U.S. accounting for $6.02 billion and Europe $5.01 billion. Growth drivers cited include rising cyberattacks, GDPR compliance, enterprise cloud adoption, and demand for zero trust architectures.

news.title: Microsoft to enable Windows settings backup by default for orgs
news.summary: Microsoft will enable Windows settings backup and restore by default for enterprise systems joined to Microsoft Entra after upgrading to Windows 11 26H2. The change affects both Microsoft Entra-joined and Microsoft Entra hybrid-joined devices, automating settings preservation across the organization.

news.title: Identifying and Using OnPremisesImmutableId from Azure AD Connect Synchronization Errors
news.summary: The article addresses resolving "Duplicate Attribute" synchronization errors in Azure AD Connect by extracting and using the OnPremisesImmutableId value directly from the error data. The technique is presented as a method to simplify hybrid identity management in Microsoft Entra/Azure AD environments.

news.title: Your company already adopted AI and nobody is governing access
news.summary: Elba Security CTO Antoine Berton discusses ungoverned access created by unsanctioned AI tool adoption within organizations. The video focuses on the AI attack surface created when employees connect AI tools to corporate systems without identity governance controls in place.

news.title: Microsoft wants to keep your AI agents from going rogue
news.summary: Microsoft has introduced Microsoft Execution Containers (MXC), a policy-driven execution layer designed to control and constrain AI agent behavior on Windows and WSL platforms. The technology applies access control and policy enforcement to AI agents, representing an extension of authorization and containment concepts into agentic AI workflows.

news.title: What does zero trust mean for mobile devices in the enterprise?
news.summary: The article examines how zero trust principles apply to mobile devices in enterprise environments. It addresses the growing threat landscape for mobile endpoints and how zero trust architectures can be used to secure corporate resources accessed via mobile devices.

news.title: Enforce least-privilege authorization in multi-agent AI chains using Cedar
news.summary: The article details using Cedar (Amazon's policy language) to enforce least-privilege authorization in multi-agent AI systems, addressing the risk of authorization scope expansion during agent-to-agent task delegation. The piece focuses on maintaining proper authorization controls in multi-hop agent chains, ensuring agents cannot act beyond what the originating user originally authorized.

news.title: ModelCop Launches AI Agent Security Platform, Targets $25B Machine Identity Market
news.summary: ModelCop has commercially launched an AI agent security platform targeting the Non-Human Identity (NHI) market, going live on July 4, 2026. The identity-first platform is designed to give enterprise security teams visibility and governance over AI agents and machine identities in a market the company values at $25 billion.

news.title: Backs Q2 Guidance at Investor Day
news.summary: SailPoint confirmed its Q2 adjusted EPS guidance of 7-8 cents against an 8-cent consensus at an investor day event on June 17, 2026. The company also reaffirmed its Q2 revenue guidance at the same event.

news.title: SailPoint (SAIL) Backs Q2 Guidance at Investor Day
news.summary: SailPoint confirmed its Q2 adjusted guidance at an Investor Day event on June 17, 2026. The announcement was noted in the context of SailPoint being listed among high-growth cybersecurity stocks.

news.title: 'Djinn' Stealer Targets Cloud, AI Credentials
news.summary: A new infostealer dubbed 'Djinn' is targeting cloud and AI credentials by exploiting CVE-2026-48558, a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in SimpleHelp. The malware specifically targets credentials connecting development and admin environments to broader enterprise systems.

news.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
news.summary: Two members of the Scattered Spider cybercrime group pleaded guilty in the UK to charges related to the August 2024 cyberattack on Transport for London. Scattered Spider is known for sophisticated social engineering and identity-based attacks, including SIM swapping and MFA bypass techniques used to compromise organizational access.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant chatbot to reset Instagram account passwords and seize accounts, including the Obama White House and U.S. Space Force accounts. Instructions circulating on Telegram demonstrated how to manipulate the AI bot into bypassing normal account authentication and recovery controls.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, prompting congressional inquiries from both chambers. CISA is actively working to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.
